title: Hepatitis B Perinatal Vaccine Information | Hepatitis B | CDC
text:
formation Statement (VIS) 
Vaccine Information Statements (VISs) are information sheets produced by CDC that explain both the benefits and risks of a vaccine. View the VIS for hepatitis B vaccine .

Content Source: 
Division of Viral Hepatitis 
Footnotes Abbreviations: HBIG = hepatitis B immune globulin; HBsAg = hepatitis B surface antigen. 
* Pregnant patients should have blood drawn and tested for HBsAg as soon as possible after admission for delivery; if the person is found to be HBsAg-positive, the infant should receive HBIG as soon as possible but no later than age 7 days. 
† Pediarix should not be administered before age 6 weeks. 
§ HBIG should be administered at a separate anatomical site from vaccine. 
¶ The final dose in the vaccine series should not be administered before age 24 weeks (164 days). 
References Schillie S, Vellozzi C, Reingold A, et al.  Prevention of Hepatitis B Virus Infection in the United States: Recommendations of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices . MMWR Recomm Rep 2018;67(No. RR-1):1–31. 
Schillie SF, Murphy TV. Seroprotection after recombinant hepatitis B v
